title: The One Where Dr. Ramoray Dies
weakest_section: 15:00-17:00 range with condom negotiation and awkward small talk

jokes.text: Doesn't she know you can't define yourself in terms of money? That it's about values and morals... ...and your ability to give and receive love?
jokes.setup: Joey watching a soap opera, commenting on a character upset about being cut out of a will
jokes.punchline: Joey delivering profound life wisdom while literally defining himself by his soap opera paycheck
jokes.explanation: Strong ironic setup given Joey's character and what we'll learn about his career concerns. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: Joey's profound monologue about values and love
jokes.punchline: Flat 'No' response from the soap opera
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Joey's speech. Perfect economy with just one word.

jokes.text: It's like Brad to have to have the last word.
jokes.setup: Dramatic soap opera moment
jokes.punchline: Joey treating ridiculous soap plot as realistic character analysis
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's investment in soap storylines as real drama. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: There's something I never told you. I'm actually your half-brother.
jokes.setup: Romantic tension between soap characters
jokes.punchline: Sudden incest revelation as plot twist
jokes.explanation: Standard soap opera parody. Predictable but functional absurdity.

jokes.text: ...where I find the world's biggest emerald. It's really big.
jokes.setup: Joey describing elaborate upcoming soap plot
jokes.punchline: Redundant clarification 'It's really big' after 'world's biggest'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey-ism. The redundancy feels authentic to his speaking pattern.

jokes.text: God, that is good TV!
jokes.setup: Absurd soap opera plot with emeralds and curses
jokes.punchline: Ross genuinely praising objectively terrible television
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic appreciation. Shows the group's support for Joey despite the show's quality.

jokes.text: Twenty armless guys joined at the waist by a steel bar... ...forced to play soccer forever?
jokes.setup: Chandler wanting to play foosball
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's literal interpretation of foosball figures as human rights violation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe moment. Takes familiar object and makes us see it completely differently through her unique perspective. Classic character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Don't feel so bad. After playing... ...I break out the plastic women and everybody has a good time.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's human rights concerns about foosball
jokes.punchline: Chandler doubling down with sexual innuendo about plastic figures
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler-style inappropriate escalation. Takes Phoebe's setup and makes it worse.

jokes.text: Oh, no! I have to go, because I'm late for... ...my Green Eggs and Ham discussion group.
jokes.setup: Chandler and Eddie about to have bonding time over beers
jokes.punchline: Eddie's transparent excuse using children's book
jokes.explanation: Establishes Eddie's awkwardness. The specificity of the excuse makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tonight, it's 'Why he would not eat them on a train.'
jokes.setup: Already absurd Green Eggs and Ham discussion group
jokes.punchline: Eddie elaborating with fake academic seriousness
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that makes the lie more elaborate and ridiculous.

jokes.text: So you think that Speed Racer guy gets a lot of tickets or...?
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to make conversation with Eddie
jokes.punchline: Desperately random conversation starter about cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Captures the awkwardness of forced small talk. Chandler trying too hard.

jokes.text: She actually thought Sean Penn was the capital of Cambodia.
jokes.setup: Eddie explaining why he broke up with someone
jokes.punchline: Absurd geographical confusion mixing actor with location
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd. The randomness of Sean Penn makes it memorable.

jokes.text: When everybody knows that the capital of Cambodia is... It's not Sean Penn.
jokes.setup: Eddie's Sean Penn/Cambodia story
jokes.punchline: Chandler's minimal but perfect response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler reaction. The pause before stating the obvious is expertly timed.

jokes.text: My last girlfriend, Tilly. We're eating breakfast, right? I made these pancakes. Like, 50 pancakes.
jokes.setup: Eddie setting up what seems like a funny story
jokes.punchline: Odd detail about making exactly 50 pancakes
jokes.explanation: The specific number is weird enough to be funny. Sets up Eddie's intensity.

jokes.text: It was literally like she had reached into my chest... ...ripped out my heart and smeared it all over my life!
jokes.setup: Eddie's 'funny' breakup story
jokes.punchline: Extremely dramatic and disturbing imagery for breakfast breakup
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Eddie's instability. The visceral imagery is both funny and concerning.

jokes.text: There was this incredible abyss and I'm falling. I keep falling and I don't think I'm ever gonna stop!
jokes.setup: Eddie's already intense breakup description
jokes.punchline: Continuing with even more dramatic metaphors
jokes.explanation: Escalates the intensity. Shows Eddie's complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: That wasn't such a funny story.
jokes.setup: Eddie's traumatic 'funny' story
jokes.punchline: Chandler's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction to Eddie's overshare. Classic Chandler deflation.

jokes.text: And a crusty old man Said I'll do what I can And the rest of the rats Played maracas
jokes.setup: Phoebe performing at coffee house
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical lyrics mixing old man, rats, and maracas
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe song weirdness. The random imagery is very her.

jokes.text: Phoebe's got another job, right?
jokes.setup: Phoebe's terrible performance ending
jokes.punchline: Ross's concerned question about her livelihood
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Shows the group's affectionate concern about Phoebe's 'talent.'

jokes.text: I don't have my jammies.
jokes.setup: Richard suggesting they stay at his place
jokes.punchline: Monica's childish word 'jammies' in romantic context
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's neuroses and the awkwardness of their age gap.

jokes.text: My baby sister, ladies and gentlemen.
jokes.setup: Monica saying 'jammies' to her older boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Ross's embarrassed commentary
jokes.explanation: Good brother embarrassment. Ross as straight man to Monica's childishness.

jokes.text: Of all the guys that Monica has been with, and that is a lot...
jokes.setup: Phoebe making a nice speech about Richard
jokes.punchline: Casual dig at Monica's dating history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - meaning to be nice but accidentally insulting. Sets up the episode's number theme.

jokes.text: She likes me best. And apparently, there have been a lot.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's comment about Monica's dating history
jokes.punchline: Richard noting both the compliment and the implication
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Richard processing the information.

jokes.text: Phoebe's dead.
jokes.setup: Phoebe embarrassing Monica in front of Richard
jokes.punchline: Monica's death threat delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan threat. Monica's competitiveness and embarrassment.

jokes.text: Can I take a message or... Or a fish tank?
jokes.setup: Someone at door asking for Eddie
jokes.punchline: Chandler noting the unusual delivery item
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler observation. The pause before 'fish tank' works well.

jokes.text: your name came up... ...in a conversation that terrified me to my very soul.
jokes.setup: Tilly asking if Eddie mentioned her
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic reference to Eddie's breakdown story
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Eddie's intensity. Chandler's dramatic phrasing mirrors Eddie's style.

jokes.text: Is Eddie a little... A little what? ...bit country? Or... You know, a little rock 'n' roll?
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to ask if Eddie is crazy
jokes.punchline: Awkward euphemisms avoiding the real question
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemisms for asking if someone is mentally unstable. The music references are random enough to work.

jokes.text: You had sex with her.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about getting a fish
jokes.punchline: Eddie's paranoid accusation out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eddie paranoia. The sudden shift from fish to accusation shows his instability.

jokes.text: That's one of my favorite digests!
jokes.setup: Joey showing Soap Opera Digest
jokes.punchline: Chandler's mock enthusiasm for 'digests' as a category
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm. The specificity of 'digests' as a reading preference is funny.

jokes.text: If we don't get this woman to a hospital, she's going to die. But I made it, 'If this woman doesn't get to a hospital... ...she's not gonna live.'
jokes.setup: Joey explaining how he rewrites his lines
jokes.punchline: The 'improved' line is virtually identical to original
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment. His pride in making a meaningless change shows his lack of self-awareness beautifully.

jokes.text: Oh, okay. I see what you did there.
jokes.setup: Joey's 'improved' identical line
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic acknowledgment of non-improvement
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Chandler enabling Joey's delusion with fake understanding.

jokes.text: Makes up most of his lines. Son of a...
jokes.setup: Writer reading Joey's interview claiming he writes his own lines
jokes.punchline: Writer's angry reaction to being discredited
jokes.explanation: Shows the consequences of Joey's bragging. Sets up his comeuppance.

jokes.text: Write this, jerk-weed. I fall down an elevator shaft?
jokes.setup: Writer angry at Joey's interview
jokes.punchline: Writer's revenge through killing Joey's character
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing real consequences. 'Jerk-weed' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: They can't kill me! I'm Francesca's long-lost son!
jokes.setup: Joey receiving his death script
jokes.punchline: Joey's desperate appeal to soap opera logic
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's investment in soap opera mythology. His desperation is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: I don't think that will affect the plot of the show.
jokes.setup: Joey refusing to sign for his death script
jokes.punchline: Delivery guy's dry observation about script vs reality
jokes.explanation: Nice reality check moment. Shows the disconnect between Joey's hopes and reality.

jokes.text: Two? Two?
jokes.setup: Richard saying he's only been with two women
jokes.punchline: Monica's shocked repetition
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective shock reaction. Sets up the number comparison theme.

jokes.text: How is that possible? I mean... ...have you seen you?
jokes.setup: Monica shocked by Richard's low number
jokes.punchline: Monica complimenting Richard while revealing her own higher number
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's directness and the awkwardness of the situation.

jokes.text: Okay, it is definitely less than a ballpark.
jokes.setup: Richard asking for ballpark figure of Monica's number
jokes.punchline: Monica taking 'ballpark' literally as measurement
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that avoids the real question while technically being accurate. Good Monica deflection.

jokes.text: Better not be doing these in order.
jokes.setup: Monica starting to list her partners with Ross first
jokes.punchline: Ross's horrified realization about chronological listing
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever observation. Ross realizing the disturbing implications of being first on the list.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, the weenie from Turinie.
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning Paolo
jokes.punchline: Ross's dismissive rhyming insult
jokes.explanation: Good Ross jealousy moment. The rhyming makes his disdain more childish and funny.

jokes.text: All it really was was just meaningless, animal sex.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to reassure Ross about Paolo
jokes.punchline: Monica's tactless description that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica tactlessness. Trying to help but making it worse with TMI.

jokes.text: Okay, you know, that sounded so much better in my head.
jokes.setup: Monica's 'animal sex' comment
jokes.punchline: Monica realizing how bad that sounded
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment. Monica recognizing her verbal fumble.

jokes.text: You sleep with my ex-girlfriend, insult my intelligence by lying... ...then you kill my fish? My Buddy?
jokes.setup: Eddie's paranoid accusations about Tilly
jokes.punchline: Escalating to accusing Chandler of fish murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Eddie's paranoia. The fish's name 'Buddy' makes it more pathetic.

jokes.text: Would you look at what I'm doing? Now that can't be smart.
jokes.setup: Chandler handling Eddie's knife
jokes.punchline: Chandler's calm observation of dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler moment. Self-aware commentary on his own dangerous behavior.

jokes.text: So we're just gonna take this guy right off you... ...and put him here in Mr. Pocket.
jokes.setup: Dangerous situation with Eddie's knife
jokes.punchline: Chandler's gentle, almost parental handling of the knife
jokes.explanation: Good tension relief. Chandler treating the knife like a child's toy is both funny and smart.

jokes.text: You had me thinking it was, like, a fleet.
jokes.setup: Richard learning Monica's actual number
jokes.punchline: Richard comparing number to naval vessels
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to earlier 'ballpark' joke with military metaphor. Shows Richard's relief and humor.

jokes.text: Or is that what you're doing with me? Oh, my God! Am I an oat?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Richard's lack of 'wild oats'
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified realization about being a wild oat
jokes.explanation: Excellent Monica panic moment. The absurd question 'Am I an oat?' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Invite Paolo to have a romp in the sack... ...and I'll stand in the corner and tell knock-knock jokes!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to reassure Ross about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Ross's bitter, self-deprecating alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ross moment. Dark humor showing his insecurity while maintaining his dignity.

jokes.text: Not even, like... ...chipmunk sex?
jokes.setup: Ross asking if they have any 'animal' connection
jokes.punchline: Ross downgrading to the smallest, least threatening animal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and escalation. The specificity of 'chipmunk sex' and Ross's desperation make this a standout moment.

jokes.text: It was good with Paolo. - Knock-knock.
jokes.setup: Monica being honest about Paolo
jokes.punchline: Ross starting an actual knock-knock joke as threatened
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback execution. Ross actually following through on his bitter joke threat.

jokes.text: There's only one.
jokes.setup: Monica and Rachel looking for condoms
jokes.punchline: Only finding one condom when both need them
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom sexual predicament. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I have a comb.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking how Richard keeps his mustache neat
jokes.punchline: Richard's matter-of-fact response to obvious question
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Richard's straightforward response to Chandler's awkward small talk.

jokes.text: I'll give this to you now, if you tell me where we keep the dustpan.
jokes.setup: Negotiating over the condom with cleaning duties
jokes.punchline: Admission of not knowing basic household item location
jokes.explanation: Shows the characters' domestically challenged nature. Decent situational humor.

jokes.text: So were you in Nam?
jokes.setup: Chandler's awkward small talk with Richard
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal question about Vietnam War
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler awkwardness. The casual way he asks about war service is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: You've got it totally the other way around, my friend. Jon Voight...
jokes.setup: Chandler correcting Richard about actors
jokes.punchline: Getting into detailed movie trivia argument
jokes.explanation: Shows male bonding over trivial details. Chandler finding his comfort zone in pop culture.

jokes.text: In the future, if I could see the schedule beforehand...
jokes.setup: Richard and Monica's romantic evening interrupted
jokes.punchline: Richard treating intimate moments like business meetings
jokes.explanation: Good Richard dry humor. His professional approach to romance scheduling.

jokes.text: So when I woke up this morning... ...he'd stolen all the insoles out of my shoes!
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining Eddie's latest bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific revenge of insole theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Eddie's weirdness. The specificity of insole theft is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Because sometimes after you sleep with someone... ...you have to kill a fish.
jokes.setup: Ross asking why Chandler would kill Eddie's fish
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation of absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic delivered deadpan. The matter-of-fact presentation of ridiculous cause-and-effect makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: I'm fine about my problem now, by the way.
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion about Ross's sexual performance anxieties
jokes.punchline: Ross casually mentioning he's solved his psychological issues
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway callback. Ross treating major psychological breakthrough as casual aside.

jokes.text: Oh, okay. All right. I love you. Yeah, whatever.
jokes.setup: Dramatic soap opera death scene
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of love declaration before death
jokes.explanation: Good soap opera parody. The casual 'yeah, whatever' response to 'I love you' before death is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Now maybe.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if they just killed off Joey after seeing him fall down elevator shaft
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry assessment of Joey's survival chances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler understatement. The timing and delivery make the obvious observation funny.

jokes.text: And some of us really have to pee!
jokes.setup: Friends trying to comfort Joey through his door
jokes.punchline: Mixing emotional support with bathroom urgency
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom bathos. Mixing the emotional with the mundane.

jokes.text: They said that when they found my body, my brain was so smashed in... ...that the only doctor who could've saved me was me.
jokes.setup: Joey explaining why his character can't come back
jokes.punchline: The absurd ironic twist of soap opera death
jokes.explanation: Good soap opera logic parody. The circular irony is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: Like, the other day, I got this credit card application... ...and I was pre-approved!
jokes.setup: Joey explaining how his soap opera success changed his life
jokes.punchline: Credit card pre-approval as life achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment. Shows his simple pleasure in mundane adult achievements.

jokes.text: You'll always be pre-approved with us.
jokes.setup: Joey's pride in being pre-approved for credit card
jokes.punchline: Chandler applying the same language to friendship
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns Joey's shallow achievement into genuine friendship moment.

jokes.text: That means nothing to me.
jokes.setup: Chandler's heartfelt friendship gesture using pre-approval language
jokes.punchline: Joey completely missing the emotional point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey response. His emotional obtuseness deflates Chandler's attempt at comfort.

jokes.text: Are these raisins? Sure, why not?
jokes.setup: Eddie offering Chandler baked goods
jokes.punchline: Eddie's casual uncertainty about his own baking ingredients
jokes.explanation: Good Eddie weirdness. His casual approach to not knowing what's in his own food.

jokes.text: I got a little surprise. Look. There's a new fishy. I named him... ...Chandler, you know, after you.
jokes.setup: Eddie trying to make amends with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Eddie naming replacement fish after his roommate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Eddie's twisted logic and attempts at friendship.

jokes.text: Well, that's not even a real fish. No, that's a Goldfish cracker.
jokes.setup: Eddie showing his 'new fish'
jokes.punchline: Reveal that it's actually a snack food
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Eddie's delusion. The specificity of 'Goldfish cracker' makes it perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: So what's your point, man?
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out the 'fish' is a cracker
jokes.punchline: Eddie's dismissive response to obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Eddie moment. His complete dismissal of reality in favor of his own narrative.

jokes.text: You big freak of nature!
jokes.setup: Eddie's completely delusional behavior throughout
jokes.punchline: Chandler finally snapping with name-calling
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for Chandler's patience finally breaking. The insult is appropriately childish.

jokes.text: Let's never speak of this.
jokes.setup: Two characters in embarrassing situation in cold
jokes.punchline: Agreement to pretend embarrassing moment never happened
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom ending beat. Functional but predictable embarrassment recovery.

tmdb_overview: Chandler learns about Eddie's quirks. Ross becomes stressed learning about Rachel's past with Paolo. Joey's magazine comments jeopardize his television role.
